description: §20.2 (Client Router — navigate-soft-nav Wave-1b). The explicit navigation-type overrides `navigate(path, .Hard)` and `navigate(path, .Soft)` compile clean: the bare-dot `.Hard` / `.Soft` variant is recognized as the navigation-type selector (NOT an undeclared identifier or a mis-inferred enum variant), so no E-SCOPE-001 and no E-VARIANT-* / E-TYPE-* fires. `.Hard` lowers to the full-document hard nav, `.Soft` to `_scrml_navigate_soft`; the variant is consumed by codegen, never forwarded as a runtime argument.
rationale: §20.2: `.Hard` / `.Soft` are built-in navigation-type variants the developer MAY use to override the inferred type. They are compiler-consumed selectors — the call site must compile without a scope/variant error.
